v.CNV: 这些事以后，耶稣渡过加利利海，就是提比里亚海。
v.ESV: After this Jesus went away to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, which is the Sea of Tiberias.
v.GAE: 그 후에 예수께서 디베랴의 갈릴리 바다 건너편으로 가시매
v.KJV: After these things Jesus went over the sea of Galilee, which is the sea of Tiberias.
v.NASB: After these things Jesus went away to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (or Tiberias).
v.NIV: Some time after this, Jesus crossed to the far shore of the Sea of Galilee (that is, the Sea of Tiberias),
v.NRSV: After this Jesus went to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, also called the Sea of Tiberias.
v.SAENEW: 그 뒤에 예수께서 갈릴리 바다 곧 디베랴 바다 건너편으로 가시니
v.WLB: 이 일이 있은 지 얼마 후에 예수께서 갈릴리 바다, 곧 디베랴 바다 건너편으로 가셨습니다.

v.CNV: 有一大群人，因为看见了他在病人身上所行的神迹，就跟随了他。
v.ESV: And a large crowd was following him, because they saw the signs that he was doing on the sick.
v.GAE: 큰 무리가 따르니 이는 병자들에게 행하시는 표적을 보았음이러라
v.KJV: And a great multitude followed him, because they saw his miracles which he did on them that were diseased.
v.NASB: A large crowd followed Him, because they saw the signs which He was performing on those who were sick.
v.NIV: and a great crowd of people followed him because they saw the signs he had performed by healing the sick.
v.NRSV: A large crowd kept following him, because they saw the signs that he was doing for the sick.
v.SAENEW: 큰 무리가 예수를 따라갔다. 그것은, 그들이 예수가 병자들을 고치신 표징들을 보았기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 그러자 환자들에게 표적을 베푸시는 것을 본 많은 무리가 예수를 따랐습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣上了山，同门徒坐在那里。
v.ESV: Jesus went up on the mountain, and there he sat down with his disciples.
v.GAE: 예수께서 산에 오르사 제자들과 함께 거기 앉으시니
v.KJV: And Jesus went up into a mountain, and there he sat with his disciples.
v.NASB: Then Jesus went up on the mountain, and there He sat down with His disciples.
v.NIV: Then Jesus went up on a mountainside and sat down with his disciples.
v.NRSV: Jesus went up the mountain and sat down there with his disciples.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 산에 올라가서, 제자들과 함께 앉으셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 산에 올라가서 제자들과 함께 앉으셨습니다.

v.CNV: 那时犹太人的逾越节近了。
v.ESV: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was at hand.
v.GAE: 마침 유대인의 명절인 유월절이 가까운지라
v.KJV: And the passover, a feast of the Jews, was nigh.
v.NASB: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was near.
v.NIV: The Jewish Passover Festival was near.
v.NRSV: Now the Passover, the festival of the Jews, was near.
v.SAENEW: 마침 유대 사람의 명절인 유월절이 가까운 때였다.
v.WLB: 그때는 유대 사람의 명절인 유월절이 가까운 때였습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣举目观看，见一大群人向他走过来，就对腓力说：“我们从哪里买饼给这些人吃呢？”
v.ESV: Lifting up his eyes, then, and seeing that a large crowd was coming toward him, Jesus said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread, so that these people may eat?”
v.GAE: 예수께서 눈을 들어 큰 무리가 자기에게로 오는 것을 보시고 빌립에게 이르시되 우리가 어디서 떡을 사서 이 사람들을 먹이겠느냐 하시니
v.KJV: When Jesus then lifted up his eyes, and saw a great company come unto him, he saith unto Philip, Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?
v.NASB: Therefore Jesus, lifting up His eyes and seeing that a large crowd was coming to Him, said to Philip, 'Where are we to buy bread, so that these may eat?'
v.NIV: When Jesus looked up and saw a great crowd coming toward him, he said to Philip, “Where shall we buy bread for these people to eat?”
v.NRSV: When he looked up and saw a large crowd coming toward him, Jesus said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread for these people to eat?”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 눈을 들어서, 큰 무리가 자기에게로 모여드는 것을 보시고, 빌립에게 말씀하셨다. "우리가 어디에서 빵을 사다가, 이 사람들을 먹이겠느냐?"
v.WLB: 예수께서 눈을 들어 많은 사람이 자기에게로 몰려오는 것을 보시고 빌립에게 말씀하셨습니다. “우리가 어디에서 빵을 사서 이 사람들을 먹이겠느냐?”

v.CNV: 他说这话，是要试验腓力，因他自己早已知道要怎样作。
v.ESV: He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he would do.
v.GAE: 이렇게 말씀하심은 친히 어떻게 하실지를 아시고 빌립을 시험하고자 하심이라
v.KJV: And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.
v.NASB: This He was saying to test him, for He Himself knew what He was intending to do.
v.NIV: He asked this only to test him, for he already had in mind what he was going to do.
v.NRSV: He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he was going to do.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 빌립을 시험해 보시고자 이렇게 말씀하신 것이었다. 예수께서는 자기가 하실 일을 잘 알고 계셨던 것이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 빌립이 어떻게 하나 보시려고 이렇게 질문하신 것일 뿐, 사실 자기가 하실 일을 미리 알고 계셨습니다.

v.CNV: 腓力回答：“就算二百银币买的饼，每人分一点，也是不够的。”
v.ESV: Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread would not be enough for each of them to get a little.”
v.GAE: 빌립이 대답하되 각 사람으로 조금씩 받게 할지라도 이백 데나리온의 떡이 부족하리이다
v.KJV: Philip answered him, Two hundred pennyworth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may take a little.
v.NASB: Philip answered Him, 'Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, for everyone to receive a little.'
v.NIV: Philip answered him, “It would take more than half a year’s wages to buy enough bread for each one to have a bite!”
v.NRSV: Philip answered him, “Six months’ wages would not buy enough bread for each of them to get a little.”
v.SAENEW: 빌립이 예수께 이렇게 대답하였다. "이 사람들에게 모두 조금씩이라도 먹게 하려면, 빵 이백 데나리온어치를 가지고서도 충분하지 못합니다."
v.WLB: 빌립이 예수께 대답했습니다. “한 사람당 조금씩만 먹는다고 해도 200데나리온 어치의 빵으로도 모자랄 것입니다.”

v.CNV: 有一个门徒，就是西门．彼得的弟弟安得烈，对耶稣说：
v.ESV: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, said to him,
v.GAE: 제자 중 하나 곧 시몬 베드로의 형제 안드레가 예수께 여짜오되
v.KJV: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, saith unto him,
v.NASB: One of His disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, said to Him,
v.NIV: Another of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, spoke up,
v.NRSV: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,
v.SAENEW: 제자 가운데 하나이며 시몬 베드로와 형제간인 안드레가 예수께 말하였다.
v.WLB: 제자들 중 하나이며 시몬 베드로의 동생인 안드레가 말했습니다.

v.CNV: “这里有个小孩子，带着五个大麦饼、两条鱼；只是分给这么多人，有什么用呢？”
v.ESV: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are they for so many?”
v.GAE: 여기 한 아이가 있어 보리떡 다섯 개와 물고기 두 마리를 가지고 있나이다 그러나 그것이 이 많은 사람에게 얼마나 되겠사옵나이까
v.KJV: There is a lad here, which hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes: but what are they among so many?
v.NASB: 'There is a lad here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these for so many people?'
v.NIV: “Here is a boy with five small barley loaves and two small fish, but how far will they go among so many?”
v.NRSV: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish. But what are they among so many people?”
v.SAENEW: "여기에 보리빵 다섯 개와 물고기 두 마리를 가지고 있는 한 아이가 있습니다. 그러나 이렇게 많은 사람에게 그것이 무슨 소용이 있겠습니까?"
v.WLB: “여기 한 소년이 보리빵 다섯 개와 물고기 두 마리를 가지고 있습니다. 그러나 이렇게 많은 사람들에게 그게 얼마나 소용이 있겠습니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣吩咐他们：“你们叫众人坐下。”原来那地方的草很多，众人就坐下，单是男人的数目约有五千。
v.ESV: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, about five thousand in number.
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 이 사람들로 앉게 하라 하시니 그 곳에 잔디가 많은지라 사람들이 앉으니 수가 오천 명쯤 되더라
v.KJV: And Jesus said, Make the men sit down. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
v.NASB: Jesus said, 'Have the people sit down.' Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
v.NIV: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” There was plenty of grass in that place, and they sat down (about five thousand men were there).
v.NRSV: Jesus said, “Make the people sit down.” Now there was a great deal of grass in the place; so they sat down, about five thousand in all.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 "사람들을 앉게 하여라" 하고 말씀하셨다. 그 곳에는 풀이 많았다. 그래서 그들이 앉았는데, 남자의 수가 오천 명쯤 되었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “사람들을 모두 앉히라.” 그곳은 넓은 풀밭이었는데, 남자들이 둘러앉으니 5,000명쯤 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣拿起饼来，祝谢了，就分给坐着的人；分鱼也是这样，都是随着他们所要的。
v.ESV: Jesus then took the loaves, and when he had given thanks, he distributed them to those who were seated. So also the fish, as much as they wanted.
v.GAE: 예수께서 떡을 가져 축사하신 후에 앉아 있는 자들에게 나눠 주시고 물고기도 그렇게 그들의 원대로 주시니라
v.KJV: And Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to them that were set down; and likewise of the fishes as much as they would.
v.NASB: Jesus then took the loaves, and having given thanks, He distributed to those who were seated; likewise also of the fish as much as they wanted.
v.NIV: Jesus then took the loaves, gave thanks, and distributed to those who were seated as much as they wanted. He did the same with the fish.
v.NRSV: Then Jesus took the loaves, and when he had given thanks, he distributed them to those who were seated; so also the fish, as much as they wanted.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 빵을 들어서 감사를 드리신 다음에, 앉은 사람들에게 나누어주시고, 물고기도 그와 같이 해서, 그들이 원하는 대로 주셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 빵을 들고 감사기도를 드리신 후 앉아 있는 사람들에게 원하는 만큼씩 나눠 주셨습니다. 물고기를 가지고도 똑같이 하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他们吃饱了之后，耶稣对门徒说：“把剩下的零碎收拾起来，免得浪费。”
v.ESV: And when they had eaten their fill, he told his disciples, “Gather up the leftover fragments, that nothing may be lost.”
v.GAE: 그들이 배부른 후에 예수께서 제자들에게 이르시되 남은 조각을 거두고 버리는 것이 없게 하라 하시므로
v.KJV: When they were filled, he said unto his disciples, Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be lost.
v.NASB: When they were filled, He said to His disciples, 'Gather up the leftover fragments so that nothing will be lost.'
v.NIV: When they had all had enough to eat, he said to his disciples, “Gather the pieces that are left over. Let nothing be wasted.”
v.NRSV: When they were satisfied, he told his disciples, “Gather up the fragments left over, so that nothing may be lost.”
v.SAENEW: 그들이 배불리 먹은 뒤에, 예수께서 제자들에게 이렇게 말씀하셨다. "남은 부스러기를 다 모으고, 조금도 버리지 말아라."
v.WLB: 그들이 모두 배불리 먹은 뒤에 예수께서 제자들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “남은 것은 하나도 버리지 말고 모아 두라.”

v.CNV: 门徒就把众人吃剩那五个大麦饼的零碎收拾起来，装满了十二个篮子。
v.ESV: So they gathered them up and filled twelve baskets with fragments from the five barley loaves left by those who had eaten.
v.GAE: 이에 거두니 보리떡 다섯 개로 먹고 남은 조각이 열두 바구니에 찼더라
v.KJV: Therefore they gathered them together, and filled twelve baskets with the fragments of the five barley loaves, which remained over and above unto them that had eaten.
v.NASB: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with fragments from the five barley loaves which were left over by those who had eaten.
v.NIV: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves left over by those who had eaten.
v.NRSV: So they gathered them up, and from the fragments of the five barley loaves, left by those who had eaten, they filled twelve baskets.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 보리빵 다섯 덩이에서, 먹고 남은 부스러기를 모으니, 열두 광주리에 가득 찼다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 그들이 남은 것을 모아 보니 보리빵 다섯 개로 먹고 남은 것이 12바구니에 가득 찼습니다.

v.CNV: 众人看见耶稣所行的神迹，就说：“这真是那要到世上来的先知。”
v.ESV: When the people saw the sign that he had done, they said, “This is indeed the Prophet who is to come into the world!”
v.GAE: 그 사람들이 예수께서 행하신 이 표적을 보고 말하되 이는 참으로 세상에 오실 그 선지자라 하더라
v.KJV: Then those men, when they had seen the miracle that Jesus did, said, This is of a truth that prophet that should come into the world.
v.NASB: Therefore when the people saw the sign which He had performed, they said, 'This is truly the Prophet who is to come into the world.'
v.NIV: After the people saw the sign Jesus performed, they began to say, “Surely this is the Prophet who is to come into the world.”
v.NRSV: When the people saw the sign that he had done, they began to say, “This is indeed the prophet who is to come into the world.”
v.SAENEW: 사람들은 예수께서 행하신 표징을 보고 "이분은 참으로 세상에 오시기로 된 그 예언자이다" 하고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 사람들은 예수께서 행하신 표적을 보고 말했습니다. “이분은 이 세상에 오신다던 그 예언자가 틀림없다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣知道群众要来强迫他作王，就独自又退到山上去了。
v.ESV: Perceiving then that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, Jesus withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
v.GAE: 그러므로 예수께서 그들이 와서 자기를 억지로 붙들어 임금으로 삼으려는 줄 아시고 다시 혼자 산으로 떠나 가시니라
v.KJV: When Jesus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force, to make him a king, he departed again into a mountain himself alone.
v.NASB: So Jesus, perceiving that they were intending to come and take Him by force to make Him king, withdrew again to the mountain by Himself alone.
v.NIV: Jesus, knowing that they intended to come and make him king by force, withdrew again to a mountain by himself.
v.NRSV: When Jesus realized that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, he withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는, 사람들이 와서 억지로 자기를 모셔다가 왕으로 삼으려고 한다는 것을 아시고, 혼자서 다시 산으로 물러가셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 그들이 와서 강제로 자기를 왕 삼으려 한다는 것을 아시고 혼자서 다시 산으로 올라가셨습니다.

v.CNV: 到了晚上，他的门徒下到海边去。
v.ESV: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
v.GAE: 저물매 제자들이 바다에 내려가서
v.KJV: And when even was now come, his disciples went down unto the sea,
v.NASB: Now when evening came, His disciples went down to the sea,
v.NIV: When evening came, his disciples went down to the lake,
v.NRSV: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
v.SAENEW: 날이 저물었을 때에, 예수의 제자들은 바다로 내려가서,
v.WLB: 날이 저물자 예수의 제자들은 바다로 내려갔습니다.

v.CNV: 他们上了船，要渡海往迦百农去。那时天已经黑了，耶稣还没有到他们那里。
v.ESV: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
v.GAE: 배를 타고 바다를 건너 가버나움으로 가는데 이미 어두웠고 예수는 아직 그들에게 오시지 아니하셨더니
v.KJV: And entered into a ship, and went over the sea toward Capernaum. And it was now dark, and Jesus was not come to them.
v.NASB: and after getting into a boat, they started to cross the sea to Capernaum. It had already become dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
v.NIV: where they got into a boat and set off across the lake for Capernaum. By now it was dark, and Jesus had not yet joined them.
v.NRSV: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
v.SAENEW: 배를 타고, 바다 건너편 가버나움으로 갔다. 이미 어두워졌는데도, 예수께서는 아직 그들이 있는 곳으로 오시지 않았다.
v.WLB: 거기서 그들은 배를 타고 바다를 건너 가버나움으로 향했습니다. 날은 이미 어두워졌고 예수께서는 아직 그들이 있는 곳으로 오시지 않았습니다.

v.CNV: 忽然海上起了狂风，波浪翻腾。
v.ESV: The sea became rough because a strong wind was blowing.
v.GAE: 큰 바람이 불어 파도가 일어나더라
v.KJV: And the sea arose by reason of a great wind that blew.
v.NASB: The sea began to be stirred up because a strong wind was blowing.
v.NIV: A strong wind was blowing and the waters grew rough.
v.NRSV: The sea became rough because a strong wind was blowing.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 큰 바람이 불고, 물결이 사나워졌다.
v.WLB: 세찬 바람이 불어 물살이 거세어졌습니다.

v.CNV: 门徒摇橹约行了五六公里，看见耶稣在海面上行走，渐渐靠近船，就害怕起来。
v.ESV: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and coming near the boat, and they were frightened.
v.GAE: 제자들이 노를 저어 십여 리쯤 가다가 예수께서 바다 위로 걸어 배에 가까이 오심을 보고 두려워하거늘
v.KJV: So when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs, they see Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing nigh unto the ship: and they were afraid.
v.NASB: Then, when they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and drawing near to the boat; and they were frightened.
v.NIV: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus approaching the boat, walking on the water; and they were frightened.
v.NRSV: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and coming near the boat, and they were terrified.
v.SAENEW: 제자들이 배를 저어서, 십여 리쯤 갔을 때였다. 그들은, 예수께서 바다 위로 걸어서 배에 가까이 오시는 것을 보고, 무서워하였다.
v.WLB: 그들이 노를 저어 한 25-30스타디온쯤 갔을 때에 예수께서 물 위를 걸어서 배 쪽으로 다가오시는 것이 보였습니다. 그들은 두려웠습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣对他们说：“是我，不要怕。”
v.ESV: But he said to them, “It is I; do not be afraid.”
v.GAE: 이르시되 내니 두려워하지 말라 하신대
v.KJV: But he saith unto them, It is I; be not afraid.
v.NASB: But He said to them, 'It is I; do not be afraid.'
v.NIV: But he said to them, “It is I; don’t be afraid.”
v.NRSV: But he said to them, “It is I; do not be afraid.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "나다. 두려워하지 말아라."
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “나다. 두려워하지 말라.”

v.CNV: 他们这才把他接上船，船就立刻到了他们要去的地方。
v.ESV: Then they were glad to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at the land to which they were going.
v.GAE: 이에 기뻐서 배로 영접하니 배는 곧 그들이 가려던 땅에 이르렀더라
v.KJV: Then they willingly received him into the ship: and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went.
v.NASB: So they were willing to receive Him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at the land to which they were going.
v.NIV: Then they were willing to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat reached the shore where they were heading.
v.NRSV: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat reached the land toward which they were going.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 그들은 기꺼이 예수를 배 안으로 모셔들였다. 배는 곧 그들이 가려던 땅에 이르렀다.
v.WLB: 그러자 그들은 기꺼이 예수를 배 안으로 모셨습니다. 배는 곧 그들이 가려던 땅에 도착했습니다.

v.CNV: 第二天，站在海那边的群众，看见只有一只小船留在那里，并且知道耶稣没有和他的门徒一同上船，门徒是自己去的；
v.ESV: On the next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the sea saw that there had been only one boat there, and that Jesus had not entered the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
v.GAE: 이튿날 바다 건너편에 서 있던 무리가 배 한 척 외에 다른 배가 거기 없는 것과 또 어제 예수께서 제자들과 함께 그 배에 오르지 아니하시고 제자들만 가는 것을 보았더니
v.KJV: The day following, when the people which stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was none other boat there, save that one whereinto his disciples were entered, and that Jesus went not with his disciples into the boat, but that his disciples were gone away alone;
v.NASB: The next day the crowd that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other small boat there, except one, and that Jesus had not entered with His disciples into the boat, but that His disciples had gone away alone.
v.NIV: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the opposite shore of the lake realized that only one boat had been there, and that Jesus had not entered it with his disciples, but that they had gone away alone.
v.NRSV: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the other side of the sea saw that there had been only one boat there. They also saw that Jesus had not got into the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
v.SAENEW: 그 다음날이었다. 바다 건너편에 서 있던 무리는, 거기에 배 한 척만 있었다는 것과, 예수께서는 제자들과 함께 그 배를 타지 않으셨고, 제자들만 따로 떠나갔다는 것을 알았다.
v.WLB: 그 다음날 건너편 바닷가에 남아 있던 많은 사람들은 그곳에 배가 한 척밖에 없었던 것과 예수께서 제자들이 탄 배에 오르시지 않고 제자들끼리 건너갔다는 것을 알았습니다.

v.CNV: 不过有几只从提比里亚来的船停在那里，靠近他们在主祝谢以后吃饼的地方。
v.ESV: Other boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
v.GAE: (그러나 디베랴에서 배들이 주께서 축사하신 후 여럿이 떡 먹던 그 곳에 가까이 왔더라)
v.KJV: (Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread, after that the Lord had given thanks:)
v.NASB: There came other small boats from Tiberias near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
v.NIV: Then some boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the people had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
v.NRSV: Then some boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 디베랴에서 온 배 몇 척이, 주님께서 감사 기도를 드리고 무리에게 빵을 먹이신 곳에 가까이 닿았다.
v.WLB: (그때 디베랴로부터 온 몇 척의 배가 주께서 감사기도를 드리고 사람들에게 빵을 먹이셨던 그곳 가까이에 닿았습니다.)

v.CNV: 群众见耶稣和门徒都不在那里，就上船往迦百农去找耶稣。
v.ESV: So when the crowd saw that Jesus was not there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats and went to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.
v.GAE: 무리가 거기에 예수도 안 계시고 제자들도 없음을 보고 곧 배들을 타고 예수를 찾으러 가버나움으로 가서
v.KJV: When the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there, neither his disciples, they also took shipping, and came to Capernaum, seeking for Jesus.
v.NASB: So when the crowd saw that Jesus was not there, nor His disciples, they themselves got into the small boats, and came to Capernaum seeking Jesus.
v.NIV: Once the crowd realized that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum in search of Jesus.
v.NRSV: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they themselves got into the boats and went to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
v.SAENEW: 무리는 거기에 예수도 안 계시고 제자들도 없는 것을 알고서, 배를 나누어 타고, 예수를 찾아 가버나움으로 갔다.
v.WLB: 그 사람들은 예수나 제자들이 모두 그곳에 없다는 사실을 알고 다시 배를 타고 예수를 찾으러 가버나움으로 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 他们在对岸找到了耶稣，就问他：“拉比，你几时到这里来的？”
v.ESV: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
v.GAE: 바다 건너편에서 만나 랍비여 언제 여기 오셨나이까 하니
v.KJV: And when they had found him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him, Rabbi, when camest thou hither?
v.NASB: When they found Him on the other side of the sea, they said to Him, 'Rabbi, when did You get here?'
v.NIV: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
v.NRSV: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
v.SAENEW: 그들은 바다 건너편에서 예수를 만나서 말하였다. "선생님, 언제 여기에 오셨습니까?"
v.WLB: 그들은 바다 건너편에서 예수를 발견하고 물었습니다. “랍비여, 언제 여기에 오셨습니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣回答：“我实实在在告诉你们，你们找我，不是因为看见了神迹，而是因为吃了饼又吃饱了。
v.ESV: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, you are seeking me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves.
v.GAE: 예수께서 대답하여 이르시되 내가 진실로 진실로 너희에게 이르노니 너희가 나를 찾는 것은 표적을 본 까닭이 아니요 떡을 먹고 배부른 까닭이로다
v.KJV: Jesus answered them and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.
v.NASB: Jesus answered them and said, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, you seek Me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves and were filled.
v.NIV: Jesus answered, “Very truly I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw the signs I performed but because you ate the loaves and had your fill.
v.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Very truly, I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 대답하셨다. "내가 진정으로 진정으로 너희에게 말한다. 너희가 나를 찾는 것은 표징을 보았기 때문이 아니라, 빵을 먹고 배가 불렀기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “내가 진실로 진실로 너희에게 말한다. 너희가 나를 찾는 까닭은 표적을 보았기 때문이 아니라 빵을 먹고 배가 불렀기 때문이다.

v.CNV: 不要为那必朽坏的食物操劳，却要为那存到永生的食物操劳，就是人子所要赐给你们的，因为人子是父　神所印证的。”
v.ESV: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For on him God the Father has set his seal.”
v.GAE: 썩을 양식을 위하여 일하지 말고 영생하도록 있는 양식을 위하여 하라 이 양식은 인자가 너희에게 주리니 인자는 아버지 하나님께서 인치신 자니라
v.KJV: Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.
v.NASB: 'Do not work for the food which perishes, but for the food which endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you, for on Him the Father, God, has set His seal.'
v.NIV: Do not work for food that spoils, but for food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For on him God the Father has placed his seal of approval.”
v.NRSV: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For it is on him that God the Father has set his seal.”
v.SAENEW: 너희는 썩어 없어질 양식을 얻으려고 일하지 말고, 영생에 이르도록 남아 있을 양식을 얻으려고 일하여라. 이 양식은, 인자가 너희에게 줄 것이다. 아버지 하나님께서 인자를 인정하셨기 때문이다."
v.WLB: 썩어 없어질 양식을 위해 일하지 말고 영생하기까지 남아 있을 양식을 위해 일하라. 인자가 너희에게 이 양식을 줄 것이다. 아버지 하나님께서 인자를 인정하셨기 때문이다.”

v.CNV: 众人又问他：“我们应该作什么，才算是作　神的工作呢？”
v.ESV: Then they said to him, “What must we do, to be doing the works of God?”
v.GAE: 그들이 묻되 우리가 어떻게 하여야 하나님의 일을 하오리이까
v.KJV: Then said they unto him, What shall we do, that we might work the works of God?
v.NASB: Therefore they said to Him, 'What shall we do, so that we may work the works of God?'
v.NIV: Then they asked him, “What must we do to do the works God requires?”
v.NRSV: Then they said to him, “What must we do to perform the works of God?”
v.SAENEW: 그들이 예수께 물었다. "우리가 무엇을 하여야 하나님의 일을 하는 것이 됩니까?"
v.WLB: 그러자 그들이 예수께 물었습니다. “우리가 어떻게 하면 하나님의 일을 하겠습니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣回答：“信　神所差来的，就是作　神的工了。”
v.ESV: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 대답하여 이르시되 하나님께서 보내신 이를 믿는 것이 하나님의 일이니라 하시니
v.KJV: Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
v.NASB: Jesus answered and said to them, 'This is the work of God, that you believe in Him whom He has sent.'
v.NIV: Jesus answered, “The work of God is this: to believe in the one he has sent.”
v.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 대답하셨다. "하나님께서 보내신 이를 믿는 것이 곧 하나님의 일이다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “하나님의 일이란 바로 하나님께서 보내신 이를 믿는 것이다.”

v.CNV: 于是他们就说：“你要行什么神迹，让我们看了就信你呢？你到底能作什么呢？
v.ESV: So they said to him, “Then what sign do you do, that we may see and believe you? What work do you perform?
v.GAE: 그들이 묻되 그러면 우리가 보고 당신을 믿도록 행하시는 표적이 무엇이니이까, 하시는 일이 무엇이니이까
v.KJV: They said therefore unto him, What sign shewest thou then, that we may see, and believe thee? what dost thou work?
v.NASB: So they said to Him, 'What then do You do for a sign, so that we may see, and believe You? What work do You perform?
v.NIV: So they asked him, “What sign then will you give that we may see it and believe you? What will you do?
v.NRSV: So they said to him, “What sign are you going to give us then, so that we may see it and believe you? What work are you performing?
v.SAENEW: 그들은 다시 물었다. "우리에게 무슨 표징을 행하셔서, 우리로 하여금 보고 당신을 믿게 하시겠습니까? 당신이 하시는 일이 무엇입니까?
v.WLB: 그러자 그들이 예수께 다시 물었습니다. “그러면 우리가 보고 믿을 수 있도록 어떤 표적을 보이시겠습니까? 무슨 일을 하시려는 것입니까?

v.CNV: 我们的祖宗在旷野吃过吗哪，正如经上所记：‘他把从天上来的食物赐给他们吃。’”
v.ESV: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
v.GAE: 기록된 바 하늘에서 그들에게 떡을 주어 먹게 하였다 함과 같이 우리 조상들은 광야에서 만나를 먹었나이다
v.KJV: Our fathers did eat manna in the desert; as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat.
v.NASB: 'Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, 'HE GAVE THEM BREAD OUT OF HEAVEN TO EAT.''
v.NIV: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written: ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
v.NRSV: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
v.SAENEW: '그는 하늘에서 빵을 내려서, 그들에게 먹게 하셨다' 한 성경 말씀대로, 우리 조상들은 광야에서 만나를 먹었습니다."
v.WLB: 우리 조상들은 광야에서 만나를 먹었습니다. 성경에 이렇게 기록됐습니다. ‘그분은 하늘에서 빵을 내려 그들에게 먹게 하셨다.’”

v.CNV: 耶稣对他们说：“我实实在在告诉你们，不是摩西把那从天上来的食物赐给你们，而是我父把天上来的真食物赐给你们；
v.ESV: Jesus then said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 내가 진실로 진실로 너희에게 이르노니 모세가 너희에게 하늘로부터 떡을 준 것이 아니라 내 아버지께서 너희에게 하늘로부터 참 떡을 주시나니
v.KJV: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Moses gave you not that bread from heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread from heaven.
v.NASB: Jesus then said to them, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, it is not Moses who has given you the bread out of heaven, but it is My Father who gives you the true bread out of heaven.
v.NIV: Jesus said to them, “Very truly I tell you, it is not Moses who has given you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.
v.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 대답하셨다. "내가 진정으로 진정으로 너희에게 말한다. 하늘에서 너희에게 빵을 내려다 주신 이는 모세가 아니다. 하늘에서 참 빵을 너희에게 주시는 분은 내 아버지시다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “내가 진실로 진실로 너희에게 말한다. 하늘에서 빵을 내려 준 분은 모세가 아니다. 오직 내 아버지께서 하늘로부터 참된 빵을 너희에게 내려 주시는 것이다.

v.CNV: 因为　神的食物就是从天上降下来，把生命赐给世人的那一位。”
v.ESV: For the bread of God is he who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
v.GAE: 하나님의 떡은 하늘에서 내려 세상에 생명을 주는 것이니라
v.KJV: For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world.
v.NASB: 'For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world.'
v.NIV: For the bread of God is the bread that comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
v.NRSV: For the bread of God is that which comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
v.SAENEW: 하나님의 빵은 하늘에서 내려와 세상에 생명을 주는 것이다."
v.WLB: 하나님의 빵은 하늘에서 내려와 세상에 생명을 주시는 것이다.”

v.CNV: 他们对耶稣说：“主啊，求你常把这食物赐给我们。”
v.ESV: They said to him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
v.GAE: 그들이 이르되 주여 이 떡을 항상 우리에게 주소서
v.KJV: Then said they unto him, Lord, evermore give us this bread.
v.NASB: Then they said to Him, 'Lord, always give us this bread.'
v.NIV: “Sir,” they said, “always give us this bread.”

v.KJV: The Jews therefore strove among themselves, saying, How can this man give us his flesh to eat?
v.NASB: Then the Jews began to argue with one another, saying, 'How can this man give us His flesh to eat?'
v.NIV: Then the Jews began to argue sharply among themselves, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
v.NRSV: The Jews then disputed among themselves, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
v.SAENEW: 그러자 유대 사람들은 서로 논란을 하면서 말하였다. "이 사람이 어떻게 우리에게 [자기] 살을 먹으라고 줄 수 있을까?"

v.CNV: 耶稣就对他们说：“我实实在在告诉你们，你们若不吃人子的肉，不喝人子的血，就没有生命在你们里面。

v.KJV: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you.
v.NASB: So Jesus said to them, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink His blood, you have no life in yourselves.

v.NRSV: So Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "내가 진정으로 진정으로 너희에게 말한다. 너희가 인자의 살을 먹지 아니하고, 또 인자의 피를 마시지 아니하면, 너희 속에는 생명이 없다.

v.CNV: 吃我肉、喝我血的，就有永生，在末日我要使他复活；
v.ESV: Whoever feeds on my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.
v.GAE: 내 살을 먹고 내 피를 마시는 자는 영생을 가졌고 마지막 날에 내가 그를 다시 살리리니
v.KJV: Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.
v.NASB: 'He who eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.
v.NIV: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.

v.WLB: 누구든지 내 살을 먹고 내 피를 마시는 사람은 영생이 있고 내가 마지막 날에 살릴 것이다.

v.CNV: 因为我的肉是真正的食物，我的血是真正的饮料。
v.ESV: For my flesh is true food, and my blood is true drink.

v.NASB: 'For My flesh is true food, and My blood is true drink.

v.NRSV: for my flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.

v.CNV: 吃我肉、喝我血的人，就住在我里面，我也住在他里面。
v.ESV: Whoever feeds on my flesh and drinks my blood abides in me, and I in him.
v.GAE: 내 살을 먹고 내 피를 마시는 자는 내 안에 거하고 나도 그의 안에 거하나니
v.KJV: He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, dwelleth in me, and I in him.
v.NASB: 'He who eats My flesh and drinks My blood abides in Me, and I in him.
v.NIV: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in them.
v.NRSV: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood abide in me, and I in them.
v.SAENEW: 내 살을 먹고, 내 피를 마시는 사람은 내 안에 있고, 나도 그 사람 안에 있다.
v.WLB: 누구든지 내 살을 먹고 내 피를 마시는 사람은 내 안에 있고 나도 그 안에 있다.

v.CNV: 正如永活的父差遣了我，我也因父活着；照样，吃我肉的人也必因我而活。
v.ESV: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever feeds on me, he also will live because of me.
v.GAE: 살아 계신 아버지께서 나를 보내시매 내가 아버지로 말미암아 사는 것 같이 나를 먹는 그 사람도 나로 말미암아 살리라
v.KJV: As the living Father hath sent me, and I live by the Father: so he that eateth me, even he shall live by me.
v.NASB: 'As the living Father sent Me, and I live because of the Father, so he who eats Me, he also will live because of Me.
v.NIV: Just as the living Father sent me and I live because of the Father, so the one who feeds on me will live because of me.
v.NRSV: Just as the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me will live because of me.

v.CNV: 这就是从天上降下来的食物，不像吗哪，你们的祖宗吃过，还是死了；吃这食物的，必活到永远。”

v.KJV: This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.

v.NIV: This is the bread that came down from heaven. Your ancestors ate manna and died, but whoever feeds on this bread will live forever.”
v.NRSV: This is the bread that came down from heaven, not like that which your ancestors ate, and they died. But the one who eats this bread will live forever.”
v.SAENEW: 이것은 하늘에서 내려온 빵이다. 이것은 너희의 조상이 먹고서도 죽은 그런 것과는 같지 아니하다. 이 빵을 먹는 사람은 영원히 살 것이다."

v.WLB: 예수의 제자들 중 여럿이 이 말씀을 듣고 말했습니다. “이 말씀은 참 어렵구나. 과연 누가 알아들을 수 있겠는가?”

v.CNV: 耶稣跟着说：“所以我对你们说过，如果不是父所赐的，没有人能到我这里来。”

v.CNV: 我们已经相信，并且知道你是　神的圣者。”

v.KJV: And we believe and are sure that thou art that Christ, the Son of the living God.
v.NASB: 'We have believed and have come to know that You are the Holy One of God.'
v.NIV: We have come to believe and to know that you are the Holy One of God.”
v.NRSV: We have come to believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.”

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“我不是拣选了你们十二个人吗？但你们中间有一个是魔鬼。”
v.ESV: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you, the twelve? And yet one of you is a devil.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 대답하시되 내가 너희 열둘을 택하지 아니하였느냐 그러나 너희 중의 한 사람은 마귀니라 하시니
v.KJV: Jesus answered them, Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?
v.NASB: Jesus answered them, 'Did I Myself not choose you, the twelve, and yet one of you is a devil?'
v.NIV: Then Jesus replied, “Have I not chosen you, the Twelve? Yet one of you is a devil!”
v.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you, the twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 대답하셨다. "내가 너희 열둘을 택하지 않았느냐? 그러나 너희 가운데서 하나는 악마이다."
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “내가 너희 열둘을 택하지 않았느냐? 그러나 너희 중 하나는 마귀다!”

v.CNV: 耶稣这话是指着加略人西门的儿子犹大说的，因为他虽然是十二门徒之一，却要出卖耶稣。
v.ESV: He spoke of Judas the son of Simon Iscariot, for he, one of the twelve, was going to betray him.
v.GAE: 이 말씀은 가룟 시몬의 아들 유다를 가리키심이라 그는 열둘 중의 하나로 예수를 팔 자러라
v.KJV: He spake of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him, being one of the twelve.
v.NASB: Now He meant Judas the son of Simon Iscariot, for he, one of the twelve, was going to betray Him.
v.NIV: (He meant Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, who, though one of the Twelve, was later to betray him.)
v.NRSV: He was speaking of Judas son of Simon Iscariot, for he, though one of the twelve, was going to betray him.
v.SAENEW: 이것은 시몬 가룟의 아들 유다를 가리켜서 하신 말씀인데, 그는 열두 제자 가운데 한 사람으로, 예수를 넘겨줄 사람이었다.
v.WLB: 이것은 가룟 시몬의 아들 유다를 두고 하신 말씀이었습니다. 그는 열두 제자 중 한 사람이었지만 나중에 예수를 배신하게 될 사람이었습니다.
